The text discusses various AI threat research attacks on classification systems, including the HopSkipJump (HSJ) attack. The HSJ attack is a query-cost-aware blackbox attack that acts on hard class labels rather than confidence levels. It finds a point on the boundary, approximates the gradient at the boundary using Monte Carlo sampling, and then takes a step in the approximated gradient direction. The process is repeated until a misclassification is achieved.
